overview.title: Compliance leadership with engineering discipline
overview.summary: A fixed-view snapshot of Timmothy D. Escolopio: regulatory readiness, cybersecurity governance, and technical fluency built from hands-on systems work.
paragraphs: Compliance and IT security programs only work when the underlying systems, controls, and evidence pipelines are real. That is the operating model I bring to regulatory readiness, operational resilience, and data governance leadership.

My work spans highly regulated financial environments, enterprise-scale data lineage and control validation, and technical implementation outside work that keeps my judgment grounded in how systems actually behave under pressure.

This profile is intentionally structured as a single workspace: select a career period on the timeline, then switch between the relevant tabs to inspect the details without ever leaving the main frame.

metrics.label: Automated tests in personal engineering work

spotlights: Zero regulatory findings at Bank of America Global Information Security.
Built audit-ready programs from scratch and drove exam readiness from 15% to audit-ready in six weeks.
Maps data risk, lineage, and control performance across thousands of enterprise applications.
Builds production-grade software and infrastructure personally, which strengthens technical judgment in GRC leadership roles.

capabilities.title: Leadership domains recruiters should see immediately
capabilities.summary: These are the operating lanes that define the value proposition: governance depth, technical fluency, and measurable delivery under scrutiny.

items.title: Enterprise GRC & Regulatory Readiness
items.description: Design and deliver governance, risk, and compliance programs aligned to FFIEC, NIST CSF, SOC 2, and ISO 27001. Translate regulatory expectations into controls, evidence, and repeatable operating processes.

items.title: Cybersecurity Program & Data Governance
items.description: Map data flows, control ownership, and quality issues across large application portfolios. Build dashboards, reporting, and control-validation structures that leadership and auditors can rely on.

items.title: AI Governance & Emerging Technology Risk
items.description: Assess how emerging systems, AI tooling, and automated decision pipelines introduce governance, privacy, and control risk. Build policy structures that are usable by technical teams, not just audit committees.

items.title: Operational Resilience & Crisis Leadership
items.description: Lead remediation planning, gap closure, evidence collection, and cross-team accountability under tight timelines. Comfortable running high-visibility workstreams where leadership needs signal, not noise.

projects.title: Personal systems work that sharpens professional judgment
projects.summary: These projects are not the main story, but they are useful evidence: they show that the compliance and security perspective is backed by real software, infrastructure, and automation experience.

items.name: Code Scalpel
items.description: MCP server toolkit for safe, targeted code analysis and modification. Includes AST parsing, symbolic execution, security scanning, and cross-file dependency analysis.

items.name: Terms Guardian
items.description: Browser-first ML extension for evaluating Terms of Service risk. Built around weighted rights categories, measurable accuracy targets, and a privacy-first architecture.

items.name: InsightForge
items.description: AI and cybersecurity trend intelligence platform using automated collection, embeddings, sentiment analysis, and content generation workflows.

items.name: Mind Wars
items.description: Containerized strategy game stack deployed as part of the wider E-Mothership infrastructure, demonstrating full-stack implementation and operations discipline.

contact.title: Reach out for resilience, governance, or security leadership conversations
contact.summary: The intended audience is recruiters, headhunters, and hiring leaders looking for someone who can lead audit-ready programs and still understand the systems underneath them.
notes: Target roles: Director or Senior Manager of Operational Resilience, BCDR Program Manager, and Compliance / IT Security leadership roles.
Best-fit conversations: regulatory readiness, data governance, cybersecurity program leadership, AI governance, and audit remediation strategy.

timeline.role: IT Security & Data Governance Consultant IV
timeline.summary: Led high-visibility audit readiness and data protection work across enterprise-scale scope, helping move a critical workstream from 15% to audit-ready in six weeks while also supporting large-scale encryption compliance visibility.
Focused on metadata analysis, risk reviews, toolkit automation, and control-gap remediation across enterprise banking systems.
Independent consulting and systems-building work focused on compliance-as-code, AI governance, and deterministic analysis approaches for regulated environments.
Established governance, data classification, and AI-control structures inside a startup environment working toward SOC 2 and ISO 27001 maturity.
Translated operational risk and reporting gaps into system requirements and improved reporting efficiency through SQL-based analytics redesign.
